title: Speech-to-Text Transcription for Voice Messages
description: Automatic server-side transcription of voice messages (OGG/Opus, max 10 min) into text. Transcription is stored alongside the audio, rendered in the message bubble with toggle. Uses a self-hosted or privacy-compliant third-party ASR engine (Whisper-compatible API). Zero audio retention beyond transcription pipeline.

steps: 1. Voice message uploaded and stored in object storage (encrypted at rest, AES-256).
2. Message created in DB with transcription_status = 'pending'.
3. Transcription job queued to dedicated ASR worker pool via message queue (e.g., BullMQ/Redis).
4. ASR worker fetches audio via pre-signed URL (TTL: 5 minutes — short to limit exposure window).
5. ASR engine (Whisper large-v3 or equivalent) processes audio, returns segments with timestamps.
6. Worker writes result to messages.transcription JSONB field, sets transcription_status = 'done'.
7. WebSocket event emitted to conversation subscribers: MESSAGE_TRANSCRIPTION_READY.

failure_states.asr_timeout: After 120s: transcription_status = 'failed'. Worker retries max 2 times with exponential backoff.
failure_states.audio_too_long: Reject at upload: voice > 600s → 413 error, no transcription attempted.
failure_states.language_detection_fail: Fallback: flag transcription_status = 'unsupported_language', do not persist garbled text.

worker_isolation.description: ASR workers run in isolated namespace with no direct DB write access beyond transcript column. Use service account with column-level Postgres privilege.
worker_isolation.network: Workers have no outbound internet access (air-gapped pod network). Whisper model loaded from internal model registry.
worker_isolation.horizontal_scaling: ASR workers scale independently via queue depth metric. Target: < 30s p95 transcription latency for 1-min audio.

messages_table_extensions.transcription: JSONB (nullable) — { text: string, segments: [{start_ms, end_ms, text}], language: string, confidence: float }

privacy: Audio never sent to external cloud ASR APIs unless explicit user opt-in AND privacy policy updated.
Transcription text treated as message content — same E2EE scope. For secret chats: transcription runs on device if E2EE, not server-side.
Users can delete transcription independently of audio: PATCH /v1/messages/{id} { delete_transcription: true } — sets transcription = NULL, status = 'none'.
GDPR/DSAR: transcription included in user data export.

security.pre_signed_url_scope: ASR worker gets read-only access to specific object key, not bucket-wide.
security.prompt_injection: ASR output treated as untrusted user-generated content. Never executed, never used as system prompt in any downstream AI. Stored as escaped text.
security.dos_vector: Bulk-uploading long voice messages to exhaust ASR queue: mitigated by per-user daily transcription quota (e.g., 60 minutes/day).

ui.message_bubble: Voice message player + 'Show transcript' toggle below waveform. Collapsed by default.

transcription_states.done: Expandable text block with detected language badge
